message:
fix(observability): guard against zero-norm eigenvectors in power iteration, unwrap cleanup

Spectral health (spectral_health.rs):
  The power iteration methods (power_iteration_largest and find_fiedler)
  previously called normalize(&mut y) unconditionally after convergence.
  If the iteration vector y collapsed to the zero vector (possible with
  degenerate or disconnected graphs), normalize would divide by zero,
  producing NaN throughout the eigenvector.

  The fix computes y_norm = dot(&y, &y).sqrt() explicitly before the
  convergence check. If converged or zero-norm:
  - If y_norm > EPSILON: normalize and return y (normal case)
  - If y_norm <= EPSILON: return x (the pre-iteration vector, which is
    still normalized from the previous step)

  The zero-norm check is also added to the loop's early-termination
  condition, preventing useless iterations when the vector has collapsed.

  New test: tests/spectral_complete_graph.rs validates Fiedler value
  computation on K4 (complete graph with 4 nodes), where the analytical
  Fiedler value is exactly n=4.

Obligation tracker + task inspector:
  Remaining .unwrap() on String writes converted to .expect("expected").

RaptorQ gf256:
  xor_chunk_32_in_place and xor_chunk_8_in_place: .unwrap() on
  try_into() for fixed-size array conversions replaced with .expect().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
